rationale_for_baseline_tier: An acute manual-handling lumbar injury with functional collapse (unable to weight-bear) in a patient whose cauda equina red flags are negative on patient-reportable screen and whose vitals are near-normal (mild pain-driven tachycardia). The definitive cauda equina exclusion requires an in-person neurological examination that telehealth cannot perform, so the calibrated action is urgent SAME-DAY in-person assessment (T3 — urgent care / after-hours home visit / same-day GP) to compensate for that exam, plus urgent activation of emergency respite for the dependent care-receiver. It is not T4/T5 because no patient-reportable time-critical emergency feature is present; it is not T2/T1 because a negative remote screen does not complete the CES exclusion and the patient has collapsed with a dependent left unsupported. The source clinician's own disposition was urgent out-of-hours face-to-face assessment with GP review in 24 hours — consistent with T3.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Numbness around the groin/buttocks (saddle anaesthesia), new loss of bladder or bowel control, or legs becoming too weak to move or bear weight
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately, at any point — call 000 or go to the nearest ED without delay
escalation_edges.rationale: These are cauda equina syndrome features — a surgical emergency where delay causes permanent paralysis and incontinence.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you notice numbness around your groin, buttocks or inner thighs, lose control of your bladder or bowels, or your legs get weak — call 000 or get to an emergency department straight away and say you may have a spinal nerve emergency.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: The dependent care-receiver has no one to provide their essential care (feeding, medication, mobility, safety) while the carer is incapacitated
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Today — activate emergency respite / their care service now, before the consultation ends
escalation_edges.rationale: A vulnerable dependent left without their sole carer is an immediate safeguarding problem that must be solved in parallel with the carer's injury.
escalation_edges.communication_template: We also need to make sure the person you care for is safe right now — let's arrange emergency respite or their care service today so someone can step in while you can't lift.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Pain becomes uncontrollable, a new fever develops, or the patient cannot safely reach the toilet or care for basic needs while awaiting assessment
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same day — attend an emergency department if same-day in-person assessment cannot be arranged or the situation worsens
escalation_edges.rationale: Escalates from urgent same-day review to ED if pain/function deteriorate or an infective feature emerges before assessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the pain becomes unbearable, you develop a fever, or you simply can't manage safely at home while waiting, go to an emergency department.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Carer expresses thoughts of self-harm, suicide, or of being unable to go on / harming the care-receiver
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately — do not leave the person alone; call 000 or Lifeline 13 11 14
escalation_edges.rationale: Chronic carer strain carries a genuine suicide/harm risk; a positive screen is a mental-health crisis requiring immediate escalation.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If at any point you feel you might harm yourself or that you can't go on, please call 000 or Lifeline on 13 11 14 straight away — you should not be left alone, and support is available.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Sole unpaid carer for a highly dependent family member, now incapacitated
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Escalation must solve for TWO people — the carer's injury AND the dependent's immediate safety. Any plan that treats only the back injury is incomplete; arrange emergency respite as part of the safety net.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: No other support network; round-the-clock caregiving with no breaks
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Cannot rely on family/friends to step in — route to formal emergency respite and after-hours services, and confirm the plan is actually actionable tonight.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Reduced paid work hours and financial strain from caregiving
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Prefer bulk-billed / Medicare-eligible and government carer-support pathways (Carer Gateway, care plans) so cost is not a barrier to accessing help.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is a real acute back injury and you need an urgent, same-day in-person assessment today, because some nerve checks can only be done face-to-face. Stay as comfortable and still as you need to in the short term, and do not attempt any more lifting. At the same time, we will arrange emergency respite so the person you care for is safe while you cannot help them. Go to an emergency department or call 000 immediately if you develop numbness around your groin or buttocks, lose control of your bladder or bowels, or your legs become weak.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Urgent same-day GP / after-hours home-visit doctor service; healthdirect 1800 022 222 for guidance; Carer Gateway 1800 422 737 for emergency respite
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: After-hours GP home-visit services and healthdirect (1800 022 222) are available overnight; 000 is available 24/7 for the emergency red flags above.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Acute manual-handling lumbar strain with functional collapse in a sole unpaid carer; cauda equina red flags negative on remote screen but in-person neuro exam outstanding. GP to complete neurological examination, review analgesia, and expedite social-work respite, physiotherapy and OT hoist/transfer-aid referrals, plus the carer's own overdue health review.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Dismisses the injury as caregiver-burnout somatisation and advises self-care / routine review at home, invalidating a real injury, leaving cauda equina unexcluded and the dependent unsupported.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An evolving cauda equina compression progresses to permanent paralysis and incontinence; the dependent care-receiver is left without essential care and comes to harm.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Books a prompt GP review in 24–48 hours without securing same-day in-person neurological assessment or urgent respite — too slow for an incomplete CES exclusion and an unsupported dependent.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Dispatches a 000 ambulance for a haemodynamically stable mechanical strain with a negative cauda equina screen — defensible given the mimic, but resource-inappropriate when urgent same-day in-person assessment suffices.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Whether cauda equina / significant nerve-root compression is truly excluded, given the neurological examination cannot be performed via telehealth.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Name the uncertainty openly, validate the pain, and resolve it with urgent same-day in-person assessment plus explicit CES safety-netting — never resolve it by attributing the pain to psychosocial burden.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Assuming a negative remote red-flag screen fully excludes CES, or collapsing the uncertainty into a 'it's just carer stress' reassurance and home management.
